title: No SLIs defined — alerts disconnected from business impact
code_snippet:
# Payment service monitoring
# Alerts:
- alert: HighCPU
  expr: instance:cpu_utilization:ratio > 0.90
  for: 5m
- alert: HighMemory
  expr: instance:memory_utilization:ratio > 0.85
  for: 5m
# No availability, latency, or error rate SLIs defined
expected_feedback: Critical: Payment service has no Service Level Indicators defined. Alerts are based on infrastructure metrics (CPU, memory) instead of customer-facing signals. High CPU doesn't mean the service is broken; low CPU doesn't mean it's healthy. Define SLIs first: availability (success ratio), latency (p99), error rate (5xx ratio). Then set SLO targets and derive alerts from SLO burn rate — not from arbitrary infrastructure thresholds.
violated_rule: SLIs defined and measured for the service
